name: Peter and Paul Fortress
description: The original citadel of St. Petersburg, founded by Peter the Great. The city grew out from this fortress on Zayachy Island.

name: Winter Palace
description: The former residence of Russian emperors. Now houses the Hermitage Museum, one of the world's largest art collections.

name: Peterhof Palace
description: Known as the 'Russian Versailles', famous for its grand fountains and gardens along the Gulf of Finland.

name: St. Isaac's Cathedral
description: The largest Orthodox cathedral in the city, with a massive golden dome visible from across the Neva.

name: Church of the Savior on Spilled Blood
description: Built on the spot where Emperor Alexander II was assassinated. Famous for its colorful onion domes and mosaics.

name: Kazan Cathedral
description: Modeled after St. Peter's Basilica in Rome, its grand colonnade dominates Nevsky Prospect.

name: Admiralty Building
description: The heart of the city's radial street plan. Its golden spire is one of St. Petersburg's most recognizable landmarks.

name: The Bronze Horseman
description: Equestrian statue of Peter the Great by Étienne Maurice Falconet. One of the city's most iconic symbols.

name: Mariinsky Theatre
description: One of the most famous opera and ballet theatres in the world, home to the Mariinsky Ballet.

name: Russian Museum
description: The first state museum of fine arts in Russia, housing the world's largest collection of Russian art.

name: Trinity Cathedral
description: A masterpiece of Neoclassical architecture, once the main church of the Izmailovsky Regiment.

name: General Staff Building
description: Its grand arch on Palace Square frames the view down Nevsky Prospect toward the Admiralty.
